title: Micromeles karchungii
taxonomy: Micromeles karchungii is a species of plant in the family Rhamnaceae. This family includes other plants such as buckthorns and California coffeeberry. The Rhamnaceae family is a part of the larger order Rhamnales, which consists of flowering plants. Rhamnales is a subgroup of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The classification of Micromeles karchungii is still under research, but it is generally classified as a member of the Rhamnaceae family.

intro: Micromeles kachungii, also known as Himalayan quince or Kachnang, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae, native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Micromeles kachungii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
